purpose: Collapsible log panel with filter buttons and bottom-anchored auto-scroll. Toggles between collapsed (just header counter) and expanded states. Shows level filters (all/info/warn/error/deviation) with per-level counts computed via memoized single-pass reduction. Auto-scrolls to bottom on new entries ONLY when user is already within 40px of the bottom (preserves manual scroll position). Each row shows timestamp, task_id, agent, level chip, message.
gotchas: Auto-scroll only triggers when the panel is open (`!isOpen` early-return); switching filters while collapsed and then opening will not snap to bottom.
Counts are computed in a useMemo over `entries` — on a long-running swarm with thousands of entries this re-runs on every entries array reference change.
FILTER_LEVELS is hard-coded; new log levels won't get a filter button without updating this constant.
patterns: Smart auto-scroll guard: `el.scrollHeight - el.scrollTop - el.clientHeight < 40` — same idiom used in ClaudeView (with 80px threshold).
Single-pass count aggregation in useMemo to avoid filtering N times for N levels.
conventions: Activate via the wrapper div className `log-panel.expanded`; the collapse/expand chevron is ⌄ (▾).
Row keying combines timestamp + index to handle duplicates.
